seoTitle: Hart vs Haslam — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hart and Haslam. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hart and Haslam.
heading: Hart vs Haslam — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hart, South Australia and Haslam, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hart has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Haslam (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hart or Haslam safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hart has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Haslam scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hart scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Haslam scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hart or Haslam?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hart has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.47% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
